channel.title: Terms on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Terms on emsenn.net

item.title: Dengbej
item.description: Dengbej (Kurmanji: dengbêj, from deng “voice” + bêj “speaker”) are Kurdish oral storytellers and singers who perform long, unaccompanied vocal narratives — epics, love stories, laments, historical accounts, genealogies, and improvisations — drawn from a communal repertoire that spans centuries. The tradition is the primary vehicle through which Kurdish history, law, kinship, and emotional life have been transmitted across generations in the absence of state institutions, written canons, or centralized literary authority.

item.title: Linguicide
item.description: Linguicide is the deliberate killing of a language by state policy. The term, coined by Amir Hassanpour (1992), distinguishes state-driven language destruction from natural language death (where a language loses speakers through demographic change) and language shift (where speakers voluntarily adopt a dominant language). Linguicide names a specific political operation: the institutions of a state — its schools, courts, media, military, civil administration — act as instruments of language killing.
